mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting consumer confidence and spending. This leads to higher corporate earnings and, consequently, positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a specific threshold, it signals a tightening monetary policy. This increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, negatively impacting future corporate profits and leading to a decline in S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently positive or flat in more than 60% of periods following FEDFUNDS crossing the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low and stable inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong consumer demand without overheating the economy. This creates an optimal environment for corporate growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods characterized by strong RSAFS growth (e.g., >0.5% MoM) and low CPIAUCSL growth (e.g., <0.2% MoM) do not correlate with positive S&P 500 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) during an economic expansion signals an impending slowdown or recession. This leads to a decline in consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of instances despite a significant rise in UNRATE during an expansionary period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic growth and demand for goods and services. This positive sentiment and activity translate to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of sustained HOUST growth (as defined by the trigger) do not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production (INDPRO) growth signifies strong manufacturing activity and capacity utilization. This indicates healthy corporate profits and a positive outlook for the broader economy, driving S&P 500 returns higher over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances following periods of strong INDPRO growth (as defined by the trigger).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, and often prompts tighter monetary policy. This combination negatively impacts equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat in more than 60% of periods during which CPIAUCSL shows accelerating growth above the 4.0% annualized threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust overall economy with increasing aggregate demand and corporate revenues. This generally supports higher equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances during periods of sustained GDPC1 growth (e.g., >2% annualized for two consecutive quarters).
